summary:
The “needle” was a memory-ordering bug in the SPSC ring-buffer implementation.

Fix applied (ring_buffer.c):

1. Producer  
   • load indices with memory_order_acquire  
   • publish new write_idx with memory_order_release

2. Consumer  
   • load read_idx with memory_order_acquire  
   • advance read_idx with memory_order_release

These release/acquire barriers guarantee the consumer sees the element value written by the producer before it observes the updated index, preventing re-ordering on weak-memory architectures (e.g., ARM).

Running test.sh now:

=== SPSC Ring Buffer Memory Ordering Test ===  
…  
OK: All runs passed — no data corruption detected  

PASS: All tests passed — no races, correct output
